novel_features.name: Cost rollup
novel_features.description: Aggregate call cost across the synced store by assistant, day, or phone number.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local SQL aggregation; the API's /analytics endpoint requires a hand-built query body for this exact roll-up.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can answer 'how much did each assistant cost last week' in one call instead of paginating /call.

novel_features.name: Transcript FTS
novel_features.description: Full-text search across every synced call transcript with assistant/date filters.
novel_features.rationale: The Vapi API has no transcript-search endpoint; FTS5 over the local store unlocks it.
novel_features.example: vapi-pp-cli transcripts search 'cancel my appointment' --since 30d --json --select id,assistantId,startedAt
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can find calls matching natural-language queries without scanning every transcript over the wire.

novel_features.name: Ended-reason histogram
novel_features.description: Group recent calls by endedReason and print counts to surface drop-offs and silence-timeouts.
novel_features.rationale: Needs local group-by across many calls; the API only returns per-call endedReason.
novel_features.why_it_matters: One call answers 'why are my calls ending today' for triage.

novel_features.name: Orphan detection
novel_features.description: List tools, files, phone numbers, and workflows that no assistant references.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-table join across synced resources; impossible without local state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can clean up unused billable resources before they accrue cost.

novel_features.name: Assistant A/B compare
novel_features.description: Side-by-side cost, average duration, and ended-reason distribution for two assistants over a window.
novel_features.rationale: Needs grouped local query; the API has no comparison endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Quick read on whether a new assistant variant is winning before fully rolling it out.

novel_features.name: Stale assistants
novel_features.description: Assistants with no calls in the last N days based on the synced store.
novel_features.rationale: Local query over the call/assistant join; the API has no inactivity filter.

novel_features.name: Bulk-call planner
novel_features.description: Read a CSV of customers and print a planned outbound batch with cost estimate; --dry-run by default.
novel_features.rationale: Agent-friendly batch surface with explicit confirm; the dashboard owns this UX in the official CLI.
novel_features.example: vapi-pp-cli calls bulk --csv customers.csv --assistant-id a1b2 --phone-number-id p9 --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents can stage and review an outbound campaign before committing.

novel_features.name: Webhook replay
novel_features.description: Re-fire the last N captured webhook events at a forward target without waiting for a real call.
novel_features.rationale: We persist a ring buffer; the official listen forwards but does not retain.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Speeds up local webhook handler iteration.

novel_features.name: Live call watch
novel_features.description: Long-poll for newly active or ended calls and print one line per event.
novel_features.rationale: Polls the synced store; agent-friendly without a websocket dependency.
novel_features.why_it_matters: A streamable handle on call lifecycle for agent supervision loops.

novel_features.name: Assistant drift
novel_features.description: Diff every assistant against a baseline assistant to see system-prompt, model, voice, and tool drift.
novel_features.rationale: Needs local pairing of full assistant configs; no API endpoint compares.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catch unintentional config drift across a fleet of assistants.

narrative.headline: Every Vapi feature, plus a local SQLite store that makes cost analytics, transcript search, and orphan cleanup possible — none of which the official CLI offers.
narrative.value_prop: Vapi is the developer platform for voice AI agents. vapi-pp-cli matches the official CLI on CRUD across all 15 resources, then adds offline analytics, transcript FTS, and a webhook replay buffer that the official tool can't touch. Every command supports --json/--select/--csv/--dry-run, and the full surface auto-mirrors to MCP for agents.
narrative.auth_narrative: Vapi uses bearer-token auth. Set VAPI_API_KEY in your environment, or run `vapi-pp-cli auth set-token <key>`. Get a key from https://dashboard.vapi.ai. The official CLI's browser-OAuth login is intentionally not duplicated; if you want it, install the upstream `vapi` for `vapi login` and let this CLI read the same token.

quickstart.comment: Bearer auth — get a key from dashboard.vapi.ai
Pull every assistant, call, tool, phone-number, workflow, and campaign into the local store
Local roll-up that the API has no preset for
Plan an outbound call before committing (use --stdin for richer customer object)

troubleshoots.symptom: 401 Invalid Key — 'private key vs public key'
troubleshoots.fix: Vapi has separate public and private keys. Use the private (server) key for this CLI; the public key is for the browser SDK.

troubleshoots.symptom: sync is slow on first run
troubleshoots.fix: Initial sync paginates every resource; subsequent runs are incremental via updatedAtGt. Use `vapi-pp-cli sync calls --since 7d` to scope the first pull.
Run `vapi-pp-cli sync calls` first — the FTS index lives in the local store, not the API.

troubleshoots.symptom: listen forwards but my handler never sees events
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm Vapi is delivering to your tunnel URL by tailing `vapi-pp-cli logs webhooks`; check `--skip-verify` if your local handler is HTTPS with a self-signed cert.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for vapi-pp-cli when an agent needs to plan or audit Vapi resources, answer cost/quality questions across many calls, search transcripts, detect orphan tools/files, or stage bulk outbound calls. For one-off browser-OAuth login or framework SDK install, the upstream `vapi` CLI is fine — they coexist.

recipes.title: Field-projected list of recent calls
recipes.explanation: Agent-native; pipe to jq or feed straight back into the LLM context with low token cost.

recipes.title: Why are my calls ending
recipes.explanation: Histogram of endedReason in one call; great for daily triage agents.

recipes.title: Find calls about a topic
recipes.explanation: FTS5 across every synced transcript with field projection — agent context-friendly.

recipes.title: Compare two assistants over a window
recipes.explanation: Cost, duration, and ended-reason distribution side by side.

recipes.title: Stage a bulk call without sending
recipes.explanation: Bulk-call planner. Provide --csv <path> --assistant-id <id> --phone-number-id <id>; default is dry-run, pass --commit to actually place them.

trigger_phrases: why are my vapi calls ending
